free_text: Neutron-nucleus total and inelastic cross sections -
3 GeV protons incident on a Pt target in pulses
1 nsec wide at 67 nsec intervals produced a neutron
beam containing a broad spectrum of neutron momenta.
Energy values are averages over momentum bins used.
Natural elements with thicknesses chosen so as to
Their uniformity in thickness and their weights were
accurate to 0.1%. Compositions were known to 0.5%.
Time-of-flight.  Data taken for several values
of solid angle in each momentum bin.
Neutron detector contained four modules, each
consisting of a veto counter, a 32 mm thick converter
and two scintillation counters for detection of
Neutron detector was located 36 m from production
target. Center line of neutron beam was 20.9 degrees
from proton direction. Attenuators were placed on
movable platforms about 106 ft from target. Detector
was located about 111 ft from target.
Cross section data were fitted to a parameterized
formula expressing cross section as a function of
four-momentum transfer. Data were then extrapolated to
zero momentum transfer (or zero solid angle).
Error due mainly to counting statistics
with uncertainty due to timing shifts added in
Possible sources of systematic uncertainties are:
